description: Advanced screening based on competitive advantage, financial physics, moat analysis, management quality, and risk resilience

screening.description: Screen all industries for companies with durable competitive advantages

I_FINANCIAL_PHYSICS.description: Does the machine work efficiently regardless of what it produces?

roe.description: Buffett: Consistent ROE > 15% over last 10 years. Proves efficient capital allocation.

grossMargin.description: Buffett: Gross margin >40% (excellent) and stable over 10 years. Volatile margins = no pricing power.

freeCashFlow.description: Buffett: Positive Owner's Earnings every year. Cash is fact, accounting profit is opinion.

oneDollarTest.description: Buffett: For every $1 retained earnings, company must create ≥$1 market value over 5 years.

maintenanceCapEx.description: Buffett: Low maintenance CapEx (<5% of revenue). Avoid 'hamster wheel' businesses.

operatingMargin.description: Operating margin >15% and stable. High SG&A or R&D costs erode advantage.

depreciation.description: Good companies have depreciation <10% of gross profit. It's a real cost.

II_THE_MOAT.description: Is the business protected from competition and commoditization?

pricingPower.description: Buffett: Must raise prices above inflation without losing market share. No 'prayer meetings' before price hikes.

notCommodity.description: Buffett: If product is commodity, must be lowest-cost producer. Otherwise, must have brand power.

uncopyableAdvantage.description: Dalio: Edge must rely on complex system/network, not single patent competitors can copy in 6 months.

switchingCosts.description: Buffett: Must be painful/expensive/risky for customers to switch. Ecosystem lock-in preferred.

understandability.description: Buffett: Must explain how company makes money in one sentence. If black box, filter out.

III_MANAGEMENT_CULTURE.description: Is the leadership rational, honest, and evolutionary?

candorInFailure.description: Dalio/Buffett: CEO must admit mistakes in annual reports. Filter out if always blames 'macro headwinds'.

resistsInstitutionalImperative.description: Buffett: Filter out companies making acquisitions/pivots because 'everyone else is' (e.g., AI hype).

skinInGame.description: Buffett: Management must own significant stock (>5%). Think like owners, not employees.

systemizedEvolution.description: Dalio: Must show history of evolving BEFORE forced to. Filter out reactive companies.

rationalCapitalAllocation.description: Buffett: Must buy back shares when cheap, issue when expensive. Filter out if buying at all-time highs.

IV_RISK_RESILIENCE.description: Can the company survive bad seasons and uncertainty?

interestCoverage.description: Buffett: Must pay interest expenses ≥5x with operating income. High debt kills great businesses.

recessionResilience.description: Dalio: Must remain profitable during last major downturns (2008, 2020). Machine must work in storms.

uncorrelatedRevenue.description: Dalio: No single client or geography >50% revenue. Diversification protects against localized shocks.

riskOfRuinZero.description: Dalio: Must hold enough cash to survive 12 months with zero revenue. Black swan protection.

debtPayoffAbility.description: Buffett: Must generate enough earnings to pay off long-term debt in max 3-4 years.

debtToEquity.description: Low debt preferred (<0.5 D/E). Adjust for buybacks as they can make ratio deceptive.

INCOME_STATEMENT_ANALYSIS.description: Deep analysis of earnings quality and cost structure

cogsAnalysis.description: Companies with advantage have consistently low COGS/Revenue ratio.

sgaConsistency.description: Wild variation in SG&A costs means no competitive advantage. Must be stable.

rdCosts.description: High R&D costs can erode advantage unless company has pricing power to pass costs.

interestExpense.description: High interest payout indicates competitive market or leveraged buyout. Filter if >20% of operating income.

netProfitTrend.description: Durable business always reports higher profit/revenue % than competitors. Exception: banks (indicates risk).

epsVsNetProfit.description: EPS can increase via buybacks. Always consider net profit trends, not just EPS.

BALANCE_SHEET_ANALYSIS.description: Asset quality, liability structure, and shareholder equity analysis

cashGeneration.description: Excess cash must come from operations, not selling bonds/equity/assets.

cashAndSecurities.description: Good sign: Lots of cash/securities with little/no debt over last 7 years.

inventoryRisk.description: Durable companies sell products that never go out of style. Low inventory obsolescence risk.

inventoryVsEarnings.description: For manufacturing: Look for both inventory AND net earnings increasing together.

netReceivables.description: Consistent decline in Net Receivables/Gross Sales % indicates competitive advantage.

currentRatio.description: Current ratio >1 generally good, but useless for companies with consistent earnings.

plantUpdateRequirement.description: Durable companies don't need to constantly update plants. Low CapEx for maintenance.

longTermDebt.description: Little/no long-term debt. If present, must be payable in <4 years of earnings.

debtMaturityProfile.description: Too much debt due at once can spook investors. Must be spread out.

retainedEarnings.description: Retained Earnings = after-tax profit - dividends - buybacks. Must grow consistently.

preferredStock.description: Good companies prefer not to have Preferred stock (very expensive).

treasuryStock.description: In India, buyback shares cancelled in 7 days. Increases returns, so be careful in analysis.

VALUATION.description: Margin of safety and intrinsic value

marginOfSafety.description: Buffett: Stock must trade at ≥25% discount to intrinsic value. Great company at wrong price = bad investment.

peRatio.description: Reasonable P/E for quality companies. Avoid overpaying.

scoring.description: Scoring based on Buffett-Dalio framework. Moat and Financial Physics weighted highest.

schedule.description: Run at 9 AM IST on weekdays

notes: This configuration implements Buffett-Dalio quality investing principles.
Focus: Durable competitive advantages, not just cheap stocks.
The system will filter aggressively - expect few companies to pass all criteria.
Those that pass are likely to be exceptional businesses worth deep research.
Adjust industry/sector filters as needed, or leave as 'All' to find best across markets.
